meta.title: Nexus System Map
meta.subtitle: As-built architecture first, with roadmap layers annotated separately
meta.summary: This page models the repo as it exists today: a Python batch engine that reads YAML configuration and source registries, polls RSS feeds, extracts article text, filters for relevance, logs per-topic events, and writes daily markdown briefings. Planned Shell, Agent, discovery, and audio capabilities are shown as roadmap overlays rather than current implementation.

layers.label: Control plane
layers.description: CLI entrypoints and configuration define what runs and which topics exist.

layers.label: Source intake
layers.description: Topic registries feed RSS polling, then URL normalization removes duplicate items.

layers.label: Runtime engine
layers.description: The daily pipeline executes ingestion, filtering, and synthesis over each topic.

layers.label: Knowledge store
layers.description: Topic-scoped YAML artifacts hold events and optional summaries for continuity.

layers.label: Outputs
layers.description: Today the engine emits markdown briefings. Future surfaces consume those artifacts.

layers.label: External model boundary
layers.description: LLM-backed stages cross an explicit provider boundary through a single client abstraction.

layers.label: Roadmap overlay
layers.description: Target-system components from design.md that are not implemented in this repo yet.

statuses.label: Implemented
statuses.meaning: Present in code and used by the current runtime path.

statuses.label: Partial
statuses.meaning: Exists in code or storage shape, but is not fully wired through the runtime.

statuses.label: Planned
statuses.meaning: Specified in design.md but not implemented in the repo.

nodes.label: CLI engine entrypoint
nodes.summary: The runtime starts through `python -m nexus engine`, which loads config, resolves the LLM client, and invokes the pipeline orchestrator.

nodes.label: NexusConfig
nodes.summary: Pydantic configuration defines the user, briefing parameters, topics, model assignments, and source behavior that drive the engine.

nodes.label: Topic source registries
nodes.summary: Each topic has a YAML registry under `data/sources/<topic>/registry.yaml` listing feed ids, URLs, tiers, and languages used during polling.

nodes.label: Global source registry
nodes.summary: A curated global feed pool exists separately and supports topic-registry generation through the `sources` CLI commands.

nodes.label: RSS polling
nodes.summary: The engine fetches and parses registered RSS feeds into `ContentItem` records with basic source metadata attached.

nodes.label: URL deduplication
nodes.summary: Tracked parameters and fragments are stripped so duplicate articles from feed variants collapse to one canonical item.

nodes.label: Article ingestion
nodes.summary: Trafilatura fetches article HTML and extracts full text, upgrading feed snippets into analyzable documents.

nodes.label: Relevance filtering
nodes.summary: Batched LLM scoring applies topic definitions to ingested items and retains only relevant articles.

nodes.label: Pipeline orchestrator
nodes.summary: The orchestrator coordinates per-topic polling, ingestion, filtering, event extraction, summary loading, and briefing generation.

nodes.label: Event extraction
nodes.summary: Relevant articles become structured `Event` entries with dates, entities, significance, and relation-to-prior fields.

nodes.label: Per-topic knowledge store
nodes.summary: Knowledge is persisted as YAML under `data/knowledge/<topic>/`; events are actively written, while weekly/monthly summaries are only loaded if present.

nodes.label: Weekly and monthly rollups
nodes.summary: Compression utilities exist and the pipeline reads rollup files for synthesis context, but the current runtime does not generate them automatically.

nodes.label: Briefing synthesis
nodes.summary: Topic contexts combine recent events, summary rollups, and top articles before an LLM writes the attributed daily markdown briefing.

nodes.label: Daily briefing artifact
nodes.summary: The current end product is a dated markdown file under `data/artifacts/briefings/`.

nodes.label: LLMClient boundary
nodes.summary: All model-backed stages route through a single provider abstraction that currently targets the Gemini SDK.

nodes.label: Shell / PWA
nodes.summary: The design spec calls for a lightweight web shell that renders briefings, timelines, chat, and audio playback, but this repo has no web runtime yet.

nodes.label: Interactive agent
nodes.summary: A chat-first agent is planned to answer questions, edit config, and perform live lookups using shared state from the engine.

nodes.label: Active source discovery
nodes.summary: The design spec includes periodic source discovery and quality scoring, but the current repo only supports curated registries and manual topic-registry building.

nodes.label: Audio + private RSS
nodes.summary: Podcast scripting, TTS, and private RSS publishing are specified in the design document but absent from the codebase.

stages.label: Load topic source registry
stages.summary: Resolve the topic slug and load `data/sources/<topic>/registry.yaml` for each configured topic.
related_nodes: Topic registries are required inputs for polling.

stages.label: Poll all feeds
stages.summary: Fetch RSS entries and build `ContentItem` records with feed-level metadata.
related_nodes: Uses poll_all_feeds from the sources layer.

stages.label: Deduplicate URLs
stages.summary: Normalize URLs to strip tracking parameters and keep the first canonical instance.

stages.label: Ingest article text
stages.summary: Fetch article HTML and extract full text. Items without extracted text are dropped from the pipeline.

stages.label: Filter by topic relevance
stages.summary: Run batched scoring against the topic definition and keep items at or above the threshold.
related_nodes: Batched LLM scoring is the first model-backed stage.

stages.label: Load prior events
stages.summary: Read the existing topic event log to give event extraction narrative continuity.

stages.label: Extract top events
stages.summary: Take the top relevant articles, ask the model for structured event objects, and collect only successful parses.
related_nodes: Caps extraction at 20 articles per topic.

stages.label: Append event log
stages.summary: Persist newly extracted events back into the topic knowledge directory.
related_nodes: This is the main persisted knowledge artifact today.

stages.label: Load summary rollups
stages.summary: Read weekly and monthly summary YAML files if they already exist; generation is not wired into the run path.
related_nodes: Compression utilities exist, but orchestration is incomplete.

stages.label: Assemble TopicContext
stages.summary: Combine config, summaries, recent events, and top articles into a synthesis-ready topic bundle.
related_nodes: This is an in-memory handoff to synthesis.

stages.label: Generate attributed briefing
stages.summary: Build a long synthesis prompt from all topic contexts and ask the configured synthesis model for final markdown.
related_nodes: This is the current terminal LLM stage.

stages.label: Write briefing artifact
stages.summary: Store the final output under `data/artifacts/briefings/<today>.md` and return its path.
related_nodes: Current output surface is markdown on disk.

data_models.label: NexusConfig
data_models.summary: Top-level runtime config used by the CLI and pipeline.
relationships: Loaded from data/config.yaml through the YAML loader.
Supplies topics and model routing for the daily pipeline.

data_models.label: GlobalSource
data_models.summary: Curated feed definition used to build topic registries.
relationships: Lives in the global registry and can be transformed into per-topic registry entries.
Drives source-health checks and manual source selection.

data_models.label: ContentItem
data_models.summary: Core article transport record passed across polling, ingestion, filtering, and synthesis selection.
relationships: Created during polling and enriched through ingestion and relevance filtering.
Top-scoring instances feed event extraction and briefing synthesis.

data_models.label: Event
data_models.summary: Structured knowledge unit representing a news development within a topic timeline.
relationships: Persisted to per-topic `events.yaml` files.
Used as narrative continuity during future extraction and synthesis.

data_models.label: Summary
data_models.summary: Compression artifact intended to roll multiple events into weekly or monthly narrative summaries.
relationships: The runtime reads summary files when present.
The compression module can generate them, but the current daily run path does not persist them.

data_models.label: TopicContext
data_models.summary: In-memory synthesis bundle combining per-topic config, summaries, events, and top articles.
relationships: Built inside the pipeline for each topic.
Consumed directly by briefing synthesis.

storage_map.label: Runtime config file
storage_map.summary: User-owned configuration file defining topics, models, and briefing preferences.
relationships: Loaded before any engine work begins.
Acts as the root of the logical data model.

storage_map.label: Global source registry file
storage_map.summary: Curated feed pool used by `python -m nexus sources` commands.
relationships: Supports topic-registry generation but is not read by the daily engine path directly.

storage_map.label: Per-topic registry files
storage_map.summary: Resolved per topic and read by the pipeline orchestrator before feed polling.
relationships: Bridges curated source selection and the live engine run.

storage_map.label: Topic event logs
storage_map.summary: Main persisted knowledge artifact, storing event timelines by topic.
relationships: Provides history for continuity and recent context for the daily brief.

storage_map.label: Weekly summary rollups
storage_map.summary: Optional summary layer that the pipeline can read if present.
relationships: Represents a partially integrated storage shape.

storage_map.label: Monthly summary rollups
storage_map.summary: Longer-horizon compression layer referenced by the synthesis prompt builder when available.
relationships: Parallel to weekly summaries but currently only a load path.

storage_map.label: Daily briefing artifacts
storage_map.summary: Final output files emitted by the engine after synthesis.
relationships: Today these markdown files are the only user-facing artifact produced by code in the repo.

architect_notes.label: Current product is batch-first, not interface-first
architect_notes.summary: The actual repo is centered on a daily engine and file outputs. The chat-first shell described in the design doc is still aspirational.
points: There is no FastAPI, messaging bot, or browser runtime in the codebase.
The only implemented end-to-end surface is markdown briefing generation.
Architecturally, the code behaves like a backend compiler with local artifacts.

architect_notes.label: Database design is a structured filesystem
architect_notes.summary: The repo stores state in YAML and markdown partitioned by topic and date. That is the current database design and should be visualized as such.
points: No relational schema or ORM exists in the repo.
The logical model is still explicit through Pydantic types and YAML shapes.
Topic isolation comes from directory structure rather than tables or collections.

architect_notes.label: Knowledge compression is present but not orchestrated
architect_notes.summary: The repo has a compression module and synthesis expects summaries, but the daily run path only loads rollups if they already exist.
points: This is the main partial subsystem in the current design.
Architecturally it means the knowledge layer has a richer intended shape than the runtime guarantees.
The visualization should separate event logging from summary compression.

architect_notes.label: Roadmap breadth exceeds implemented depth
architect_notes.summary: The design document specifies discovery, breaking news, agent chat, web shell, RSS, and audio, but they are not represented in the runtime code yet.
points: The site should help an architect distinguish code truth from target-state ambition.
Roadmap components should be treated as adjacent planned systems, not hidden assumptions inside the current graph.

main_cli.label: CLI entrypoints
main_cli.summary: Defines `engine` and `sources` commands, config loading, LLM initialization, and pipeline invocation.

config_models.label: Config models
config_models.summary: Pydantic configuration and runtime typing surface.

config_loader.label: Config loader
config_loader.summary: Loads YAML from disk and validates it into `NexusConfig`.

data_config.label: Runtime config example
data_config.summary: Current instance configuration with three tracked topics and model assignments.

pipeline.label: Pipeline orchestrator
pipeline.summary: Defines per-topic execution order and the final briefing write path.

polling.label: RSS polling
polling.summary: Parses feeds into `ContentItem` objects with source metadata.

dedup.label: Deduplication
dedup.summary: Normalizes URLs and removes duplicates before ingestion.

ingest.label: Article ingestion
ingest.summary: Fetches pages and extracts full text with Trafilatura.

filter.label: Relevance filtering
filter.summary: Runs batch and fallback scoring through the LLM client.

events.label: Event logging
events.summary: Defines `Event`, YAML persistence, and model-backed extraction.

compression.label: Knowledge compression
compression.summary: Defines summary rollup structures and LLM compression helpers.

briefing.label: Briefing synthesis
briefing.summary: Builds TopicContext prompt material and generates the final markdown briefing.

llm_client.label: LLM boundary
llm_client.summary: Provider abstraction currently backed by the Gemini SDK and keyed by model roles.

registry_module.label: Source registry module
registry_module.summary: Loads curated global sources, filters them by topic, and builds per-topic registries.

global_registry_data.label: Global registry data
global_registry_data.summary: Curated multilingual source catalog with tiers, tags, affiliation, and country.

topic_registry_example.label: Topic registry example
topic_registry_example.summary: Concrete example of a topic-specific feed registry used by the live engine.

knowledge_example.label: Knowledge artifact example
knowledge_example.summary: Shows the actual persisted event log shape with summaries, entities, sources, and significance.

briefing_example.label: Briefing artifact example
briefing_example.summary: Real generated markdown output demonstrating the current product surface.

design_shell.label: Shell / PWA roadmap
design_shell.summary: Design document sections describing the web shell, dashboard, and topic timelines.

design_agent.label: Agent roadmap
design_agent.summary: Design document sections describing chat-first interaction, config edits, and contextual Q&A.

design_discovery.label: Source discovery roadmap
design_discovery.summary: Design document sections describing active source discovery and tiered source architecture.

design_audio.label: Audio and RSS roadmap
design_audio.summary: Design document sections describing dialogue scripts, TTS, audio publishing, and private RSS delivery.

claude_doc.label: Project state notes
claude_doc.summary: Documents the current phase and explicitly marks PWA, Telegram agent, audio, and discovery as deferred.

state_doc.label: Session state
state_doc.summary: Summarizes what has been built and the next implementation steps.
